observations.recommendation: We recommended the Management to direct the Accountant to prepare and submit the monthly BRS for each bank account in a timely manner along with supporting documents such as bank statements, transaction records, and any relevant correspondence.

We recommended the Management to:

Ensure that PPAs funded out of the 20% EDF are implemented within the period specified in the AIP. For PAPs that are still unimplemented and ongoing, positive efforts should be made to fast track the implementation of the PAPs;

Ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PAPs identified in AIP; and

Ensure that development projects are well-planned and are procurement-and-implementation-ready.

We recommended the Management to adhere strictly to COA Circular 97-002 and Section 93 of PD 1445 requiring payments for government transactions to be made in check and payable directly to the creditor or a cash advance shall be granted to the disbursing officers for a specific purpose. Stop the practice of disbursing government funds thru reimbursement to officials who earlier paid the transaction in cash. Only Disbursing Officers are authorized to pay in cash through the cash advance issued to them. Also, management should ensure that the proper procurement procedures are implemented by the Department Head responsible for procurement.

We recommended the Management to:

Submit all perfected contracts and purchase orders together with the complete supporting documents to COA within five working days upon approval thereof for the immediate review of the reasonableness of the cost, conduct of inspection and other necessary audit procedures.

Direct and ensure that the preparers/end-users properly accomplish the Purchase Requests, where all procurement information found in the Purchase Orders are based, indicating all the vital information required as provided in Section B of COA Circular No 96-010 and Section 5.2 of COA Circular 97-004 to promote effective and efficient operations of the agency.

We recommended the Management to direct the Municipal GSO to furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of the AIR within the prescribed period of 24 hours after the receipt, and acceptance of the consumable items and after the inspection has been conducted by the LGU Inspector of the items received.

Hereafter, perform a detailed cost-benefit analysis comparing the expenses of renting Lights and Sounds for various events to the potential costs and benefits of procuring the equipment by assessing the agency's long-term needs for Lights and Sounds and whether frequent usage justifies the procurement of the equipment. Evaluate the past rental expenses for Lights and Sounds to understand the total costs incurred and assess the frequency and scale of events where such equipment is required.

Integrate the outcomes of the analysis into the agency's budgetary planning, making adjustments to future allocations based on the analysis.

Submit to COA the cost-benefit analysis of future rental transactions or other similar transactions to be undertaken by the agency.

We recommended the Management to establish a designated and secured storage area for the tire inventory. Additionally, implement proper storage racks or covers to shield the tires from environmental factors, and enhance security measures to prevent the tires from unnecessary loss and premature deterioration. This will ensure optimal tire conditions and operational efficiency.

We recommended the Management to:

Implement a regular maintenance schedule to ensure that heavy equipment are properly maintained and in good working condition. This includes routine inspections, lubrication, and preventive maintenance tasks. By following the schedule, the LGU can identify and address potential issues before they become major problems.

Maintain accurate records of all repairs, maintenance activities, and equipment history.  This can help the LGU identify recurring issues and provide a history of equipment’s upkeep.

Implement timely repair policy for its heavy equipment to ensure their optimal performance and longevity. Timely repairs can also prevent further damage and the need for costly repairs or equipment replacement.

We recommended that management:

Require all officials and employees to liquidate their cash advances immediately and institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned officials or employees by withholding or suspension of their salaries; and

Require the Municipal Accountant to send final demand letters to those persons who have retired or out of service who still have unliquidated cash advances even to those whose whereabouts are already unknown (in that case send the letter to the last known address of the person;

File request for write off for those dormant unliquidated CAs, and AOs who are already dead or with unknown whereabouts, after gathering the required documents to support that the condition for write off as provided in COA Circular 2016-005; and

Moving forward, strictly implement that all cash advances granted within the year should be liquidated at yearend.

We recommend that Management:

Ensure that PAPs funded out of the 20% EDF are implemented within the period specified in the AIP. For PAPs that are still unimplemented and ongoing, positive efforts should be made to fast track the implementation of the PAPs;

Ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PAPs identified in AIP; and

Ensure that development projects are well-planned and are procurement-and-implementation-ready.

We recommended that Management be informed and updated of the relevant issuances by authorities that may affect their local governance performance scorecards like the DILG’s Seal of Good Local Governance and comply with the provisions therein like the submission of PSCP.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to ensure that the:
Municipal Accountant:
Present the current portion of Loans Payable account under Financial/ Current Liabilities in the Statement of Financial Position;
Disclose pertinent loan details in the NFS in keeping with IPSAS 1.
Submit a separate and updated Schedule of Loan Amortization as basis for the Finance Committee in the preparation of the LGU’s Annual Budget and for the Accounting Office to make the necessary adjustments of the recorded loan amortizations, to show the correct amounts actually paid for the principal and the loan interest.
Reconcile variances with the Bank and make necessary adjustments in the books to present the actual outstandings loan balance as of year-end; and
Local Finance Committee:
Maintain special accounts for every loan obtained using the proper account codes as prescribed presently in the Revised Chart of Accounts for LGUs and subsidiary ledgers under COA Circular No. 2015-009 dated December 1, 2015.
Ensure that all loan related transactions and documents are properly kept and maintained.
Obtain bank confirmation balances as deemed necessary to facilitate reliable reconciliation;

We recommended that Management ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Send out demand letters to all accountable officers to liquidate their cash advances immediately.
Institute appropriate legal sanction against concerned accountable officers, or by withholding/suspension of the salaries of active accountable officers.
Develop a strategic way to expedite liquidation of cash advances of officers and employees who have already died or no longer in service, exhausting all efforts to have these accounts settled and when no other alternative procedures are available, management may file a request for write-off.
Comply with the requirements set forth under COA Circular No. 2016 - 005 dated December 19, 2016 which provides for the Guidelines and Procedures on the Write-off of Dormant Receivable Accounts, Unliquidated Cash Advances, and Fund Transfers of National Government Agencies, Local Government Units and Government-Owned and Controlled Corporations when filing for the request for authority to write-off dormant accounts.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Makes the necessary adjusting entries to correct the errors; and
Moving forward, thoroughly review each transaction related to the procurement of construction materials, ensuring their accurate recording in the books of accounts, in alignment with the guidelines set forth by IPSAS 17 and to the accounts defined in COA Circular No. 2015-009.
We also recommended and the Management agreed to ensure the MEO submit a monthly report on the actual usage of materials purchased for projects implemented by the administration to the Municipal Accountant’s Office for proper accounting of inventories and related construction in progress.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant:
Adequately and accurately disclose the information required by IPSASs and other COA issuances in the NFS which are not presented on the face of the FS (such as the reconciliation of the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities and Surplus (Deficit) ) to provide additional information relevant to an understanding of any of them.
Prepare a reconciliation of the Net Cash Flows from Operating Activities and Surplus (Deficit) and investigate the identified discrepancy and submit to the Auditor a report of any adjustments made as a result of the reconciliation.

We recommended and Management agreed to ensure that the Municipal Accountant adequately and accurately disclose the information required by IPSASs and other COA issuances in the Notes to FS which are not presented on the face of the financial statements (such as Reconciliation between the SCBAA and the Statement of Financial Performance) to provide additional information relevant to an understanding of any of them.

We recommended that Management facilitate the conduct of the one-time cleansing of PPE by performing the following:
Identify other departments within the LGU that may have available staff with relevant skills (e.g., record-keeping, accounting, or logistics) who can be temporarily reassigned to assist and focus with the inventory process.
Collaborate with other government agencies that can provide training on inventory management, especially on the full implementation of the 'One-time Cleansing of PPE Accounts,' as outlined in COA Circular No. 2020-006, so that the LGU may be fully aware of the primary guidelines of the said circular.
Establish a new set of Inventory Committee, with members temporarily relieved of their regular duties until the completion of the physical inventory within a clear and agreed-upon timeline.
Ensure that Inventory Committee to assign a unique Property Number for each PPE item following the required code format in the numbering system as provided in Section 5 of the Circular.
Ensure that the newly reconstituted Inventory Committee prepare and submit the approved Physical Inventory Plan to the COA Audit Team at least ten (10) calendar days before the scheduled start of the inventory-taking process.

We recommended and the Management agreed that:
The MEO, in coordination with the Municipal Planning and Development Office (MPDO) and other relevant departments, establish a robust Monitoring and Evaluation system designed to regularly track project progress and performance, enabling the early identification of potential bottlenecks.
The LGU will invest in building the capacity of local government personnel through targeted training programs to enhance their project management skills ensuring that the MEO is better equipped to oversee projects effectively and efficiently.

We recommended and the Management agreed to comply with COA Circular No. 97-002 and Section 93 of PD No. 1445 by:
Ensuring that all payments be made payable directly to the creditor to whom the money is due.
Allowing only authorized Disbursing Officers to make cash payments using the cash advances issued to them.
Ensuring proper procurement procedures are followed.

We recommended that Management:
Submit immediately to the Audit Team and post on the agency’s website, the AAPSI for the CY 2023 AAR in accordance with the requirements of Section 99 of the General Provisions of GAA FY 2023; and,
Designate a responsible official to oversee the preparation, update, and consolidation of the status of implementation of recommendations ensuring that in all future periods, the AAPSI is submitted on time.

We recommended and the Management agreed to ensure that the GSO, in coordination with the MEO, to take the following actions:
Implement a formal system, manual or electronic for logging daily usage of all heavy equipment with detailed records of equipment operating hours, tasks performed, and locations served.
Designate a staff member or team to be responsible for the daily logging of equipment usage, ensuring consistency and accuracy ensuring that the logs are verified as to their completeness.
Provide training for equipment operators on the importance of proper recordkeeping and the correct procedures for logging usage data.

We recommended and the Management agreed  that the GFPS:
Set the GAD agenda or identify priority gender issues to be addressed by the LGU during the term of its leadership through the issuance of an executive order/memorandum for the preparation of the GAD Agenda.
Attend capacity building trainings to enhance technical knowledge on GAD and apply concepts learned in the LGU.

We recommended that Management ensure that the
GAD GFPS and the MPDO spearhead the setup and maintenance of the GAD database to serve as a basis for gender-responsive planning, programming, and policy formulation.
Undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and to ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issue.
We recommended that the GFPS undertake gender analysis to identify and confirm existing gender issues and ensure that the programs, projects and activities are responsive to the said issues.

We recommended and the management agreed to ensure:
Any unutilized amount in the trust fund of DRRM after five years be reverted to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund to be made available for other social services after subsequent enactment by the Local Sanggunian.
Any unexpended QRF and DRRM Fund- MOOE of the current year be transferred before the end of the same year.

We recommended the Management to instruct the Municipal Accountant to make the necessary adjusting entries to correct the overstatements and understatements of the aforementioned accounts. This will improve the fair presentation of these accounts in the Statement of Financial Position. Additionally, proper classification of accounts should be observed when recording transactions to maintain accurate financial records.

We recommended the Management to:

Municipal Accountant facilitate the maintenance of special account in the General Fund for the 20% development fund as mandated under Section 313 of RA 7160.

Municipal Treasurer consider opening a separate bank account for the 20% EDF since this fund has material balances and constant reconciliation of balances per actual cash back up and appropriation is necessary for the attainment of the objectives of which fund was created.
We recommended the Management to direct the BAC and all concerned End-Users to ensure that correct mode of procurement is used in the procurement of office supplies and/or equipment, moving forward.

We recommended the Management to cease allowing transactions to be processed and paid without the approval of designated officials responsible for ensuring compliance with laws, rules, and regulations.

We recommended the Management to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct a comprehensive review of requests from employees intending to monetize 50% or more of their accrued leave credits (VL & SL). This entails demanding valid and justifiable reasons and ensuring compliance with additional requirements outlined in Section 23 of the Omnibus Rule before authorizing any disbursements;

Require the personnel listed above to furnish justification and verifiable evidence demonstrating that the proceeds were utilized for purposes outlined in the Omnibus Rule; and,

Moving forward ensure compliance with Rule XVI of the Omnibus Rules Implementing Book V of E.O. No. 292

We recommended the Management to direct the Municipal Treasurer to submit RCDs and RCIs to the Municipal Accountant on a regular basis so that the latter can also prepare the journal entry vouchers to be submitted to the Auditor within the prescribed period.

We recommended the Management to fully institutionalize the MDRRMO by filling the statutory/mandatory positions required under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular 2014-1 to ensure that DRRM programs, projects and activities will be implemented in an effective, efficient and economical manner.

We recommended the Management to:

Plan procurement meticulously by considering actual needs, past consumption data, expiration dates, current inventory levels, and usage rates—particularly for items with limited shelf life. This will help prevent overstocking and reduce the risk of deterioration and wastage of government funds;

Provide a detailed explanation or justification for why the expired medical supplies valued at P2,285,556.00 should not be disallowed in the audit; and

Properly dispose of the expired inventory items and ensure adherence to existing policies for documentation during the disposal process.

We recommended that Management direct the GFPS to attend GAD seminars and training to capacitate them to execute GAD related laws and regulations, including the concept and application of GMEF in determining the LGU’s level of GAD mainstreaming and identifying steps to improve its GAD mainstreaming efforts.

Require the Municipal Accountant to send final demand letters to those persons who have retired or out of service who still have unliquidated cash advances even to those whose whereabouts are already unknown (in that case send the letter to the last known address of the person); 

File request for write off for those dormant unliquidated CAs, and AOs who are already dead or with unknown whereabouts, after gathering the required documents to support that the condition for write off as provided in COA Circular 2016-005; and

Moving forward, strictly implement that all cash advances granted within the year should be liquidated at yearend.

Stop the practice of disbursing government funds thru reimbursement to officials who earlier paid the transaction in cash. Only Disbursing Officers are authorized to pay in cash through the cash advance issued to them.

Also, management should ensure that the proper procurement procedures are implemented by the Department Head responsible for procurement.

Ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PAPs identified in AIP; and 

Ensure that development projects are well-planned and are procurement-and-implementation-ready.

We recommended that Management establish an Internal Audit Service (IAS) office with sufficient support from the top Management to gain the cooperation/ confidence of the auditee in compliance with AO No. 70, NGICS, and Section 124 of PD 1445.

We recommended that the Management require the LDRRMC to include in the Annual LDRRMF Investment Plan and incorporate in the Local Development Plan all programs, projects, and activities (PPAs) to be charged to prior years unexpended DRRMF in the special trust fund.

We recommended that the LGU comply with the pertinent requirements and regulations concerning the DILG-LGSF-AM Projects.

Also, the Local Chief Executive direct the Municipal Accountant to prepare and submit all the required reports.

We recommended that Management direct the GFPS to attend GAD seminars and training to capacitate them to execute GAD related laws and regulations, including the concept and application of GMEF in determining the LGU’s level of GAD mainstreaming and identifying steps to improve its GAD mainstreaming efforts.

We recommended that Management be informed and updated of the relevant issuances by authorities that may affect their local governance performance scorecards like the DILG’s Seal of Good Local Governance and comply with the provisions therein like the submission of PSCP.

Management Comment
During purchase, the pandemic was high. It was difficult to travel in and out the municipality without the screening so the RHU anticipated and purchased that much quantity of kits. Unfortunately, weeks later when the stocks arrived, the restrictions were lifted and travelers were quite free to move in and out. They were unable to anticipate that restrictions would be lifted that early since the pandemic is still high.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to review the documents and MOAs (Memorandum of Agreements) relative to the fund transfers so that the pertinent reports will be submitted and any unused balance can be returned to the source agency or remitted to the BTr.

We recommended that management stop the practice of providing separate appropriations for extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses and discretionary expenses and limit allocations and expenditures for these expenses within the 2% limitation prescribed under Section 325(h) of R.A. 7160. Compute the budget for these expenses using the actual real property tax collection in the next preceding year.

We recommended that Management:

Require the Municipal Accountant to thoroughly compute the correct amounts of taxes that should have been withheld and deducted from the employees and officials, and reflect the same to the alpha list to be submitted to BIR, 

Refund any excess and deduct any deficiency in the taxes withheld.

Hereafter, ensure that correct amount of taxes should be withheld by performing annualization of employees’ compensation income following the Income Tax Provisions of RA No. 10963 or the TRAIN Law in order to avoid over or under remittance to the BIR.

We recommended Management to:

Direct the Municipal Accountant not to process payrolls for RATA without the  above required documents; and

Direct the payroll officer  to properly compute for the allowable amount due to each claimant based on the submitted 1.) Certification that the official/employee did not use government vehicle and is not assigned any government vehicle and 2.) Certificate or evidence of service rendered or approved DTR with complete attachments pursuant to the provisions in the Local Budget Circular No. 103 dated May 15, 2013.

We recommended and the management agreed to undertake the following:

Exercise prudence in the disbursement of public funds and abidance to the relevant rules and law. Certification of and Approval of Disbursement Vouchers by the authorized officers for payments of salaries and wages (cash advances or not) should not be made when supporting documents are incomplete so that payment could not be facilitated;

Require the Human Resource Management Officer to deduct the incurred undertimes and absences of the employees from their leave credits and attach the certification of the same to the payroll, or require the Municipal Accountant to directly deduct these incurrences from their salaries and wages, whichever is applicable; and

No payment shall be made to any worker on the corresponding period of his absence without approved leave.

We recommended and the management agreed to:

Require the concerned department heads to prepare and submit its AAPSI concerning the audit observations and recommendations stated in LGU's ARR for CY 2020;

Moving forward, require all department heads to prepare and submit AAPSI for the following years using the prescribed form shown under COA Memorandum No. 2014-002 dated March 18, 2014; and

Designate an employee who will consolidate the report before submission to the audit team.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant:

effect the necessary correcting entries to correct the erroneous recording using the following pro-forma entry:
submit to the office of the Resident Auditor the copy of the JEV for the corrections made; and
moving forward, evaluate properly all repairs or improvements done on the properties of the LGU so it will be correctly recorded in the books.

We recommended that management: 

cause the immediate refund of the salaries received by the Municipal Mayor which was irregularly paid to her in the period of her Maternity Leave; and
moving forward, disallow employees who would avail Maternity Leave and paid in full at the time of application to return to work if the prescribed period is not fully enjoyed with. Notwithstanding, if she returns to work, exclude her in the preparation of the payroll because she is not anymore entitled with the payment of services rendered on the days within her maternity leave period.

We recommended that the Management:

discontinue the practice of not following the RA No. 9184 in the procurement of services related to the Celebration of Araw ng Kabasalan and any other similar activities of the LGU; and 
carry out procurement process for regularly programmed projects and activities strictly in conformity with RA No. 9184 for the best interest and objective utilization of the resources of the municipality.

We recommended that management should take proper action on Government properties that are for disposal. The General Services Officer should prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report of Unserviceable Property (IIRUP) and undertake the necessary actions in accordance with Section 79 of PD 1445. Subsequently, submit the necessary reports to the Accounting Office so that they can record the disposal transactions and/or effect necessary adjusting entries if needed and furnished the Office of the Auditor the copies of journal entry vouchers made.

We recommended that Management provide a garage that will shelter these LGU acquired vehicles and protect from the natural elements to prolong its useful life. Also, we recommended that management to speed up the disposal process of those vehicles that are already beyond economic repair.

We recommended that the BAC strictly follow the guidelines on resorting to Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement as provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure that the appropriate mode of procurement was employed.

We recommended that management

direct the Bids and Awards Committee to strictly implement RA 9184 in procuring goods and services of the LGU;
direct the Bids and Awards Committee to schedule the procurement in accordance with the Annual Procurement Plan so that the procurement procedures of goods and services that will be used for the Activities will be performed before it will be utilized or consumed; and
ensure that the Attendance Sheets are properly and completely filled up.

We recommended that management submit the Monthly Report of Fuel Consumption and the Report of Official Travels as prescribed by the abovementioned guidelines to facilitate the validation of the reasonableness of gasoline consumption of the Municipality. Also, direct all Office Heads and Drivers to ensure the submission of complete DTTs with the required serial number and completely filled out with necessary information for every trip undertaken.

We recommended that management refrain from charging expenditures against the MPOC Fund that are not within the definition of POP as define above and/or not included in the priority PPAs of the LGU based on the crafted POPS Plan.

We recommended that management prepare the Report on Monthly Utilization of BGCM and the Status of Implementation of PPAs using the prescribed format from the date it was received by the LGU until to date, submit these reports to the Office of the Auditor, and post the same to the three designated public conspicuous places of the Municipality with proper and visible identification.

We recommended that management direct the Municipal General Services Officer (MGSO) to furnish the Office of the Auditor a copy of the Acceptance and Inspection Report (AIR) within the prescribed period of 24 hours after the receipt, and acceptance of the consumable items and after the inspection has been conducted by the LGU Inspector of the items received.
We recommended that management instruct the GSO to have the vehicles of the Municipality marked “FOR OFFICIAL USE ONLY” with the name of the department/office where such vehicle has been issued

We recommended that management shall look or search for some probable alternatives before conducting or implementing any programs, projects, and activities; evaluate the effectiveness, efficiency, and economic viability of every alternative; and exercise due diligence in choosing the alternative that will generate or give the best possible results or benefits to the government and to the public.

We recommended that Management fully institutionalize the MDRRMO by filling the statutory/mandatory positions required under NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC Joint Memorandum Circular 2014-1 to ensure that DRRM programs, projects and activities will be implemented in an effective, efficient and economical manner.

We recommended that appropriation for MOOE and CO for the Office of the MDRRMO be included in the annual budget of the municipality and to refrain from charging to LDRRMF, expenditures which are not related to disaster risk management activities to provide sufficient funds for the DRRM programs.

We recommended that Management require the Municipal Accountant the following:
Reclassify those PPE items included in the overall cost of the e-Governance System previously recorded in lump in the Computer Software account and submit the copy of the Journal Entry Voucher to the Office of the Auditor attached the breakdown of every reclassified items;
Assess the procured Computer Software Systems to determine its useful life and record the amortization for the year 2021; and
Comply with the disclosure requirements as prescribed by IPSAS 31.

We recommended that Management:
submit to the office of the Auditor the Physical Inventory Plan;

moving forward, discontinue the practice of directly entering into an agreement with the supplier of goods so that equal opportunity will be given to other qualified suppliers and services will be procured at the most advantageous price.

We recommended that management stop utilizing the LDRRMF for expenditures of the Municipal DRRM Office and support staffs, and program/projects/activities (PPAs) which are not directly with disaster prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response and rehabilitation and recovery in adherence with Item 5.0 of NDRRMC-DBM-DILG JMC No. 2013-1, item 4.0 of NDRRMC-DILG-DBM-CSC JMC No. 2014-1, and Section 21 of RA 101211 for more implementation of LDRRM related PPAs

We recommended that management immediately correct the noted deficiencies to ensure the safety of the occupants of the Buildings owned and so that no fines, penalties, abatements or closures are imposed on the agency.

We recommended that management shall establish GAD monitoring and evaluation system and that includes the Local Chief Executive to issue Administrative Order (AO) creating the GAD M&E Team as required under Section 3.4 and 5.1 of PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that management shall strictly adhere to Item V of COA Circular No. 2014-001 dated March 18, 2014 for the timely submission of the said reports to the COA Audit Team and to Item C.8 of PCW-DBM-DILG-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 for the preparation and further guidelines on the submission of the GAD Accomplishment Report. Likewise, submit the lacking pertinent reports/documents to be attached to the submitted GAD AR of your LGU.
We recommended that management shall ensure that HGDG tool specifically the PIMME GAD checklist-Box 16 and 17 are administered to the identified GAD PPAs in the GPB during the preparation of GAD AR.

We recommended that official concerned be directed to comply with the aforementioned COA Circular to facilitate timely review and evaluation of the job order contracts and contracts of services of the LGU.
We recommended that the concerned accountable officers be directed to submit final reports of their accountabilities to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that Management procure supplies, materials and equipment directly from the PS and secure the CNAS before procuring them from local suppliers in accordance with the provisions of AO No. 17 and the Revised IRR of RA 9184.

We recommended that Management observe the modes of procurement provided under the Revised IRR of RA 9184 even in transactions which are immediately necessary. All procurement activities must be in accordance with the specific law, rules and regulations.

We recommended the BAC to strictly follow the guidelines on resorting to Shopping as an alternative mode of procurement as provided in the 2016 Revised IRR of RA 9184 to ensure that the appropriate mode of procurement was employed.

We recommended that management revise the contracts and job orders of the personnel and incorporate changes to eliminate the provisions that are violating the CSC Resolutions and RA 7160 and add stipulations that are required by the said rules and regulations. Also, to those hired with duties and functions that are clerical or administrative in nature or where the work is also performed by the regular personnel of the agency, their employment period should only begin when the exigency of the work commences and ends when such exigency ceases.

We recommended that the LGU conduct review and assessment for each LEE to determine which must continue to operate and which must adopt exit strategies like divestment, service shedding, and closure to maximize the earnings potential of the municipality.

We recommended that:
management exercise due diligence of a good father of a family in authorizing employee to travel for the purpose of attending seminars, trainings, conferences, conventions, and any other equivalent;
the Municipal Accountant be directed to scrutinize the Invitation Letter or Call thoroughly before approving disbursing vouchers related to the travel herein described especially when the transactions are involving Cash Advances for travel; and
management should ensure LGU’s compliance to the EO 77, series of 2019 in granting or authorizing travel to employees and approving DVs relative to the travel to avoid unnecessary disbursements of government funds.

We recommended that management immediately discontinue the granting of honoraria to the concerned LGU employees and to refrain from paying claims of this nature in the future to avoid disallowances in audit. In addition, management is enjoined to adhere to existing rules and regulations in the grant of honoraria to ensure the legality of transactions. It should be noted that agency heads shall be held responsible and personally liable for honoraria payments not in accordance with the provisions of above-cited circulars.

We recommended that Management:
Ensure that PAPs funded out of the 20% EDF and implemented within the period specified in the AIP. For PAPs that are still unimplemented and on-going, positive efforts should be made to fast track the completion of the PAPs; and
Ensure close monitoring of the implementation of the PAPs identified in the AIP.

We recommended that management ensure that the Municipal Gender and Planning activity shall be in accordance with PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2016-01 dated January 12, 2016, PCW‐NEDA‐DBM Joint Circular 2012‐01, and RA 9710 – Magna Carta of Women Act of 2009 so that DILG will duly endorse it. 

Moreover, we recommend that the Local Finance Committee to ensure that before the municipal GPB be integrated in the Annual Budget, this shall be endorsed by the DILG.

We recommended that the Accountant to immediately make a detailed review of the accounts with abnormal balances, determine the cause/s of the same, and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the affected accounts.  Henceforth, exercise due care in recording, analyzing and classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions. A copy of the JEV taking up the adjustments shall be furnished to the Office of the Auditor.

We recommended that management not to authorize deductions from the salaries of the   employees that will reduce their net take home pay below P5,000.00 as prescribed by the law. Deductions from salaries should be prioritized as shown above.

We recommended that management observe the limitations for extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses prescribed under Section 325(h) of R.A. 7160. Further, expenses chargeable to specific accounts must not be charged under the extraordinary and miscellaneous expenses account.

We recommended to Management the following:  
a.) To strictly observe prudence in the use of government funds for meals and snacks by ensuring that these are utilized only for expenses which are regular and necessary; b.) Discontinue the provision of meals and snacks during the conduct of regular meetings and activities which are embedded in the regular functions and responsibilities of the concerned officials and employees; c.) Keep expenses within the budget; and d.) Stop the practice of charging Representation Expenses to other accounts.

We recommended that management refrain from providing meals and snacks to LGU officials during the conduct of regular meetings or the performance of their respective functions. In cases where meals and snacks are provided, the same shall be charged against the RATA of the officers concerned.

We recommended that the HRMO and the Office of the Municipal Accountant must refrain from granting monetization of leave credits in excess of 30 days in a given year except in meritorious cases as provided under Sections 22 and 23 of the Omnibus Rules on Leave.

We recommend that Management require the Treasurer to find ways on how to facilitate the preparation and submission of the duly certified list showing the names of all taxpayers and the tax due from each taxpayer at the start of the year to the Accountant as a basis of recording in the books of accounts the RPT/SET Receivables.  Moreover, the Accountant should make a detailed analysis of the Receivable accounts and their contra accounts to determine the cause/s of the discrepancies and effect the necessary adjustments in the books of accounts.

We recommend that the Municipal Treasurer to send written notices to the payees of the existence of the checks and to require the latter to return the stale checks as basis of the Municipal Accountant for its cancellation and adjustment.
We recommend that Management provide a garage that will shelter these LGU acquired vehicles and protect from the natural elements to prolong its useful life.

We recommended that management:


           a. Require all officials and employees to liquidate  their  cash
              advances immediately;


           b. Monitor the compliance  of  AOMs  issued  by  the  ICFAU  for
              liquidation of CF;


           c.  Institute  appropriate  legal  sanction  against   concerned
              officials or employees, by withholding or suspension of their
              salaries; and

           d.  Comply and implement Items 6.0 and 7.0 of COA  Circular  No.
              2012-004 dated November 28, 2012 with subject:   “Demand  for
              Immediate Liquidation and Settlement  of  All  Cash  Advances
              Outstanding as of December 31, 2011.

   Management Comment:

   the LGU will comply with the recommendations provided in the AOMs  issued
   by the ICFAU.

We recommended that the management direct the  Inventory  Committee

   and the GSO to maintain  the  stock  &  property  cards  so  that  proper
   reconciliation of the recorded Inventories and PPE between the accounting
   records and the result of physical count can be made.

   Management Comment:

        The General Services Office is currently updating the  records  and

   prepare the reports on the conduct of physical count.

   assets. Also, there is a need to determine the condition of  the  PPE  as

We recommended  that  management  to  monitor  the  status  of  the

   concerned  the  immediate  liquidation  or  implementation  in  cases  of
   unutilized fund transfers.

   persons/entities. On the other hand, the Municipal Accountant  to  verify

   possible write-off  taking  into  consideration  the  provisions  of  COA
   Circular 2016-005 and the preparation of the  documents  to  support  the
   request for authority to write-off from the COA.


   Management Comment:

        The Municipal Accountant will  enforce  collection  from  concerned
   persons/entities  and  analyze  the  receivables  for  the  request   for
   authority to write-off dormant accounts.

We reiterated our recommendation that the Accountant to immediately
   make a detailed review of the accounts with abnormal balances,  determine
   the cause/s of the same, and effect the necessary adjustments/corrections
   in the books of accounts to present the correct balances of the  affected
   accounts.  Henceforth, exercise due  care  in  recording,  analyzing  and
   classifying the Municipality’s financial transactions. A copy of the  JEV
   taking up the adjustments  shall  be  furnished  to  the  Office  of  the
   Auditor.


   Management Comment:


        The Municipal Accountant will  make  a  detailed  review  on  these
   abnormal accounts appearing in the subsidiary ledgers and make  necessary
   adjustments in the books of accounts of the municipality.
